sanskrit:
तत्र सत्त्वं निर्मलत्वात्प्रकाशकमनामयम्।
सुखसङ्गेन बध्नाति ज्ञानसङ्गेन चानघ॥
transliteration:
tatra sattvaṃ nirmalatvāt prakāśakam anāmayam |
sukha-saṅgena badhnāti jñāna-saṅgena cānagha ||
literal_meaning: There, the mode of goodness—because of its purity—illuminates, is healthy; it binds through attachment to happiness and to knowledge, O sinless one.
contextual_meaning: Even the clearest, most virtuous state of mind (sattva) can become a subtle cage when we over-identify with feeling good or being right.
psychological_state: The ‘positive-ego’ trap—feeling wise, pure, or accomplished keeps one looping in subtle attachment rather than moving toward liberation.
trigger_scenario: A wellness influencer who feels spiritually elevated and judges others for ‘lower vibrations’, unaware that the very self-image of being ‘high-vibe’ is a binding attachment.
anchor_text: Let goodness guide you, not chain you to comfort or certainty.
